_metadata.description: Comprehensive mapping of Microsoft 365 capabilities to ACSC Essential Eight controls and maturity levels, sourced from official Microsoft compliance documentation.

purview_compliance_manager.description: Premium templates for Essential Eight at all three maturity levels for automated monitoring, continuous assessment, and configuration drift detection.
purview_compliance_manager.template_location: Purview Compliance Manager > Templates > Asia Pacific > Essential Eight

m365_license_maps.note: Community project by Microsoft employees, not official Microsoft documents

_metadata.copilot_note: Copilot for Microsoft 365 inherits existing Essential Eight controls and posture. Currently will not operate on Macro-enabled Office documents.

controls.description: Ensures only approved code such as executables, software libraries, scripts, installers, and drivers is authorized to execute.

ML1.description: Application control on workstations using AppLocker with path-based rules.
key_configurations: Deploy AppLocker policies via Intune or Group Policy
Block execution from C:\Windows\Temp\*.*
Block execution from %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\*.* (with exception for WindowsApps)
Block execution from %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Roaming\*.*
Use AaronLocker (Microsoft open-source) for policy creation
Enable Application Identity service

ML2.description: WDAC on workstations and internet-facing servers with recommended block rules, centralized logging.
key_configurations: Deploy WDAC policies via Intune in multiple policy format
Implement Microsoft recommended application blocklist (ISM-1544)
Configure managed installer for Intune
Enable WDAC script enforcement
Configure centralized logging via Defender for Endpoint P2 and Sentinel
Implement WDAC audit mode first, then enforcement mode
Use WDAC Wizard for policy creation
Validate application control rulesets annually (ISM-1582)

ML3.description: WDAC on all workstations, internet-facing and non-internet-facing servers, with driver restrictions and full logging.
key_configurations: Extend WDAC to non-internet-facing servers (ISM-1656)
Restrict driver execution to organization-approved set (ISM-1658)
Enable Hypervisor-protected code integrity (default on Windows 11 2022+)
Implement Microsoft vulnerable driver blocklist (ISM-1659)
Event logs from workstations analyzed in timely manner (ISM-0109)
Event logs from non-internet-facing servers analyzed (ISM-1907)

controls.licensing: WDAC available on supported Windows 10/11 editions. Defender for Endpoint P2 for advanced hunting. Microsoft Sentinel for SIEM.

controls.description: Ensuring applications are patched in a timely manner to reduce exposure to known vulnerabilities.

ML1.description: Vulnerability scanning and patching of productivity suites, browsers, email clients, PDF software, and security products within 2 weeks.
key_configurations: Onboard devices to Defender for Endpoint for continuous vulnerability scanning (ISM-1698, ISM-1699)
Configure Windows Update for Business with update rings for 2-week patching (ISM-1691)
Use automated asset discovery at least fortnightly (ISM-1807)
Use vulnerability scanner with up-to-date database (ISM-1808)
Remove unsupported applications via Intune (ISM-1704)
Remove unsupported online services (ISM-1905)
Apply critical patches for online services within 48 hours (ISM-1876)

ML2.description: Extended patching to all other applications within 1 month, plus fortnightly scanning for non-core apps.
key_configurations: All ML1 configurations plus:
Patch all other applications within 1 month (ISM-1693)
Fortnightly vulnerability scanning for non-core applications (ISM-1700)
Use Intune Enterprise Application Management for app lifecycle
Configure DVM-Intune integration for remediation workflows
Enable Microsoft Intune connection in Defender portal: Settings > Endpoints > General > Advanced features

ML3.description: Critical patches within 48 hours, removal of all unsupported applications, and extended vulnerability management.
key_configurations: All ML2 configurations plus:
Critical patches for productivity suites/browsers/email within 48 hours (ISM-1692)
Non-critical patches for productivity suites within 2 weeks (ISM-1901)
Remove all unsupported applications including non-core (ISM-0304)
Use Microsoft Defender vulnerability management for identification
Use Intune application deployment for rapid patch deployment

controls.licensing: Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management (standalone or Defender for Endpoint P2 with DVM add-on). Intune Enterprise Application Management requires Intune Suite license.

controls.description: Controlling the execution of Microsoft Office macros to prevent malicious code execution.

ML1.description: Block macros from internet, disable for non-business users, enable antivirus scanning of macros.
key_configurations: Import and deploy ACSC Office Hardening Guidelines policy via Intune
Block macros from running in Office files from the internet (ISM-1488)
Disable macros for users without demonstrated business need (ISM-1671)
Deploy 'All Macros Disabled' policy to All Office Users
Ensure macro security settings cannot be changed by users (ISM-1489)
Enable macro antivirus scanning: Force Runtime AV Scan = Enabled (ISM-1672)
Deploy OLE package activation prevention script

ML2.description: All ML1 controls plus trusted publisher enforcement and logging.
key_configurations: All ML1 configurations plus:
Disable Trust Bar Notification for unsigned applications (ISM-1675)
Block Win32 API calls from Office macros via ASR rule 92E97FA1 (ISM-1673)
Deploy ASR rules in audit mode first, then enforce
Onboard devices to Defender for Endpoint for macro execution logging

ML3.description: All ML2 controls plus Trusted Publisher enforcement, annual validation, and signed macros only.
key_configurations: All ML2 configurations plus:
Only allow macros signed by Trusted Publisher (ISM-1674)
Deploy 'Macros Enabled for Trusted Publishers' policy for exempted users
Add Trusted Publisher certificates via Intune
Validate Trusted Publisher list annually (ISM-1676)
Check macro code before signing (ISM-1890) on disconnected device with ACSC policies
Only privileged users can write to Trusted Locations (ISM-1487)
Use V3 signature scheme for signing macros

controls.description: Hardening user-facing applications to reduce the attack surface against malicious websites, emails, and removable media.

ML1.description: Basic browser hardening, disable IE11, remove legacy components.
key_configurations: Deploy Microsoft Edge Security Baseline via Intune Endpoint Security
Deploy ACSC Microsoft Edge Hardening Guidelines via Intune
Block web advertisements: 'Ads setting for sites with intrusive ads' = Enabled (ISM-1485)
Java not installed by default on Windows 10/11 (ISM-1486)
Disable or remove Internet Explorer 11 (ISM-1666)
Configure IE mode for legacy compatibility sites
Remove .NET Framework 3.5 via UserApplicationHardening-RemoveFeatures.ps1 (ISM-1655)
Remove Windows PowerShell 2.0 via script (ISM-1612)

ML2.description: All ML1 plus ASR rules for Office, OLE prevention, security settings enforcement.
key_configurations: All ML1 configurations plus:
Block Office from creating child processes via ASR rule D4F940AB (ISM-1667)
Block Office from creating executable content via ASR rule 3B576869 (ISM-1668)
Block Office from injecting code into other processes via ASR rule 75668C1F (ISM-1669)
Prevent activation of OLE packages via PowerShell script (ISM-1542)
Block Adobe Reader from creating child processes via ASR (ISM-1670)
Ensure web browser, Office and PDF security settings cannot be changed by users (ISM-1585)
Deploy ACSC Office Hardening Guidelines

ML3.description: All ML2 plus constrained language mode, centralized logging, command line process creation logging.
key_configurations: All ML2 configurations plus:
Enable PowerShell Constrained Language Mode (via Application Control)
Centrally log blocked PowerShell script executions (ISM-1664, ISM-1665)
Log command line process creation events (ISM-1899)
Onboard devices to Defender for Endpoint for centralized logging
Application Control event IDs captured by Defender for Endpoint (ISM-1405)

controls.description: Restricting privileged access using a multi-layered approach to increase the difficulty for adversaries.

ML1.description: Separate privileged accounts, conditional access for admins, login restrictions, internet/email restrictions for privileged accounts.
key_configurations: Assign dedicated privileged accounts for admin duties (ISM-0445)
Privileged accounts must be cloud-only, not synced from on-premises AD
Remove Microsoft 365 licenses from privileged accounts to block email/productivity tools (ISM-1175)
Configure Conditional Access requiring MFA for admin roles
Block access at High Sign-in Risk and High User Risk from Identity Protection
Require access from Intune-compliant Microsoft Entra joined device
Implement Microsoft Tiered Administration Model for login restrictions (ISM-1688, ISM-1689)
Validate requests for privileged access via Entra Entitlement Management (ISM-1507)
Separate privileged and unprivileged operating environments (ISM-1380)
Restrict privileged accounts from accessing internet from PAW via proxy configuration

ML2.description: ML1 plus managed local admin passwords, service account management, inactive user management, SAW for admin activities, centralized logging.
key_configurations: All ML1 configurations plus:
Deploy Microsoft LAPS for local administrator password management (ISM-1648)
Use Group Managed Service Accounts (gMSAs) for AD-joined systems
Use Azure Managed Identities for cloud-based service accounts
Disable privileged access after 45 days of inactivity using Microsoft Graph API lastSignInDateTime (ISM-1648)
Revalidate privileged access every 12 months via Entitlement Management (ISM-1647)
Centrally log privileged access events via Entra audit/sign-in logs to Log Analytics (ISM-1509, ISM-1650)
Use Secure Admin Workstations for administrative activities (ISM-1898)
Configure Intune EPM: deny all requests not defined in policy, require business justification and Windows authentication
Secure intermediaries using Azure Bastion and Entra application proxy

ML3.description: ML2 plus JIT access via PIM, access reviews, Credential Guard, Remote Credential Guard, full SAW deployment.
key_configurations: All ML2 configurations plus:
Configure Microsoft Entra PIM for just-in-time access with approval workflow (ISM-1508)
Require PIM elevation for: Global Admin, Privileged Auth Admin, Privileged Role Admin, CA Admin, Intune Admin
Require PIM for Azure RBAC: Owner and Contributor roles
Configure break glass accounts with strong passwords, cloud-only, monitored sign-ins
Conduct regular Microsoft Entra access reviews
Enable Credential Guard (default Windows 11 22H2+) (ISM-1686)
Enable Remote Credential Guard via Intune settings catalog (ISM-1897)
Deploy full Secure Admin Workstations with Defender for Endpoint, Windows Hello, Entra ID
Monitor privileged access events for signs of compromise
Protect event logs from unauthorized modification using RBAC and immutable storage

epm_settings.reporting_scope: Diagnostic data and all endpoint elevations
epm_settings.note: EPM requires Intune Suite add-on license

monitoring_events: Sign-in failure at bad password threshold
Failure due to Conditional Access requirements
New devices or locations for admin sign-ins

controls.description: Patching operating systems in a timely manner to reduce exposure to known vulnerabilities.

ML1.description: OS patches for internet-facing servers within 2 weeks (noncritical) and 48 hours (critical). Workstation patches within 1 month.
key_configurations: Configure Windows Update for Business with 5-ring deployment model
Ring 0 (Test): 0-day deferral for dedicated test devices
Ring 1 (Pilot): 2-day deferral, 1% of devices
Ring 2 (Fast): 4-day deferral, 9% of devices
Ring 3 (Broad): 7-day deferral, 90% of devices
Ring 4 (Critical): 10-14 day deferral for executive/critical devices
Use WUfB expedited quality updates for critical/zero-day patches (ISM-1877)
Daily vulnerability scanning for internet-facing servers (ISM-1701)
Fortnightly vulnerability scanning for workstations (ISM-1702)
Replace unsupported operating systems (ISM-1501)

ML2.description: Same as ML1 with additional scanning requirements.
key_configurations: All ML1 configurations
Enroll devices in Windows Autopatch for automated update management
Autopatch deployment rings: Test, First, Fast, Broad
Configure Delivery Optimization for bandwidth management

ML3.description: ML2 plus 48-hour patching for critical workstation OS, driver and firmware patching.
key_configurations: All ML2 configurations plus:
Critical workstation OS patches within 48 hours (ISM-1696)
Non-critical workstation patches within 1 month (ISM-1902)
Driver patches within 48 hours for critical (ISM-1879), 1 month for noncritical (ISM-1697)
Firmware patches within 48 hours for critical (ISM-1903), 1 month for noncritical (ISM-1904)
Use Intune Drivers and Firmware update management
Configure automatic or manual driver approval in Intune
Fortnightly firmware vulnerability scanning (ISM-1900)
Fortnightly driver vulnerability scanning (ISM-1703)

controls.licensing: Windows Update for Business included with Windows 10/11. Windows Autopatch requires E3+. Intune Drivers/Firmware management requires Intune license.

controls.description: Implementing MFA to make it more difficult for adversaries to steal credentials and gain access.

ML1.description: MFA for all users using any supported authenticator type.
key_configurations: Enable MFA for all users via Conditional Access policies
Supported authenticator types include: Microsoft Authenticator (push), phone-based OTP, software tokens
Block legacy authentication protocols that cannot enforce MFA
Configure Security Defaults as minimum baseline (free tier)

ML2.description: Phishing-resistant MFA using cryptographic authenticators that bind output to session.
key_configurations: Deploy phishing-resistant MFA methods that cryptographically verify the sign-in server
Configure Windows Hello for Business with biometrics or PIN + TPM-bound key
Deploy FIDO2 security keys
Microsoft Authenticator with number matching and additional context
Disable manual-entry authenticator methods (OTP, SMS) for phishing resistance
Configure Conditional Access to require phishing-resistant MFA
ML2.phishing_resistance_note: Authenticators involving manual entry of output (OTP, SMS) are NOT considered verifier impersonation-resistant per NIST SP800-63B.

ML3.description: Phishing-resistant MFA for all users with identity verification for credential issuance.
key_configurations: All ML2 configurations enforced for all users
Use Microsoft Entra Verified ID for high-assurance identity verification during credential issuance
Partner with identity verification provider (IDV) for government-issued ID verification
Use Face Check with Microsoft Entra Verified ID for real-time facial matching
Enforce phishing-resistant methods only via Conditional Access authentication strength policies

controls.nist_reference: NIST Special Publication 800-63 B - Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Lifecycle Management

controls.description: Ensuring important data is protected from loss through regular backups with appropriate retention and recovery testing.

ML1.description: Backups performed and retained per business criticality. Restoration tested as part of disaster recovery. Unprivileged users cannot access or modify others' backups.
key_configurations: Configure Microsoft Purview retention policies for Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, Yammer (ISM-1511)
Test restoration of data, applications, settings from backups (ISM-1515)
Synchronize backups to enable common point-in-time restoration (ISM-1810)
Retain backups securely and resiliently (ISM-1811)
Configure RBAC so unprivileged users cannot access others' backups (ISM-1812)
Prevent unprivileged users from modifying/deleting backups (ISM-1814)
Use in-place retention as primary strategy for M365 data
Configure SharePoint/OneDrive versioning and recycle bin
Configure Exchange Online in-place hold or litigation hold
Use retention labels for granular item-level retention

ML2.description: ML1 plus privileged user backup access restrictions.
key_configurations: All ML1 configurations plus:
Prevent privileged accounts (except backup admins) from accessing others' backups (ISM-1705)
Prevent privileged accounts (except backup admins) from modifying/deleting backups (ISM-1707)
Prevent unprivileged users from accessing even their own backups (ISM-1813)
Configure Azure Backup with RBAC roles: Backup Contributor, Backup Operator, Backup Reader
Use Azure Backup Resource Guard for multi-user authorization on critical operations
Configure PIM for backup admin role elevation with approval workflow

ML3.description: ML2 plus prevent privileged users from accessing own backups and immutable retention for backup admins.
key_configurations: All ML2 configurations plus:
Prevent privileged accounts (except backup admins) from accessing their own backups (ISM-1706)
Prevent backup admins from modifying/deleting backups during retention period (ISM-1708)
Use Immutable Storage for Azure Blob Storage for tamper-proof backup retention
Use Azure Backup multi-user authorization with Resource Guard in separate subscription/tenant

backup_approaches.in_place_retention: Primary approach for M365 data using Purview retention policies and labels
backup_approaches.azure_backup: For on-premises and Azure-hosted workloads (VMs, SQL, files)
backup_approaches.mars_agent: For file/folder/system state backup from on-premises or VMs
backup_approaches.infrastructure_as_code: For cloud service configuration backup and disaster recovery
backup_approaches.windows_autopilot: For device configuration recovery without traditional backup

controls.ransomware_considerations: OneDrive for Business file restore and SharePoint versioning provide point-in-time recovery. Exchange Online supports item-level recovery from deleted items.

secure_score_mappings.note: Microsoft Secure Score controls align to Essential Eight through keyword and functional mapping. The following shows which Secure Score control categories map to E8 controls.

mandatory_requirements.pspf_section_14_2: Essential Eight (Maturity Level 2) is mandatory for all Australian noncorporate Commonwealth entities subject to the PGPA Act.
